seoTitle: How to Create 300 DPI Images in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Set up print-ready 300 DPI designs in Canva and export them correctly for brochures, merchandise, and professional print materials.
intro: Print materials need a higher resolution than screen graphics, and 300 DPI is the standard for professional-quality prints. This guide covers setting up a design at that resolution in Canva and exporting it correctly. It's aimed at freelancers, marketers, and business owners producing brochures, merchandise, or other printed materials.

steps.heading: Set up your Canva design
steps.body: Create a new design and configure the dimensions with print resolution in mind.

steps.heading: Understand DPI and its importance
steps.body: Know that higher DPI means sharper printed output, which matters most for physical materials rather than screens.

steps.heading: Export your image correctly
steps.body: Choose export settings that preserve the 300 DPI resolution when downloading your file.

steps.heading: Apply to business use cases
steps.body: Use the print-ready image for brochures, merchandise, or other materials that need professional print quality.

faq.question: Why does DPI matter for printing but not for web use?
faq.answer: Screens display images at fixed pixel resolutions regardless of DPI, while printers rely on DPI to determine sharpness on paper.

faq.question: Does Canva always export at 300 DPI by default?
faq.answer: Export settings can vary, so it's worth checking your download options to confirm the resolution before printing.

faq.question: Can I convert an existing low-resolution design to 300 DPI?
faq.answer: Simply changing the DPI setting won't add detail that isn't there — starting with high-resolution source images gives the best results.
